note.text: The weekly headcount export shows a mismatch against the source field, so the validation rule needs a correction path before the review meeting.

label_rationale: Numbers in an exported report do not match the underlying source, which is a data-quality defect rather than a broken feature or a request.

note.text: A pilot coordinator requests the standard admin login setup for a new reviewer before the scheduled onboarding session.

label_rationale: The note is a routine request to set up standard sign-in for a new reviewer ahead of an onboarding session; it is a calm administrative access request, so the tone is neutral.

note.text: The nightly data sync between the field app and the planning dashboard drops records, so the two systems no longer agree by morning.

label_rationale: Data fails to move reliably between two connected systems, which is an integration reliability problem rather than a fault in a single screen.

note.text: Saving a custom operations dashboard throws an error and the change fails after a page reload for every user on the team.

label_rationale: A save action raises an error and does not persist, a defect on one screen; the note reports a broken workflow, so the tone is negative, and a concrete next step is already implied.

note.text: The new export filter that groups open items by owner works well and the team finds it helpful for the weekly stand-up.

label_rationale: The team describes a reporting capability they value and speak well of, so it reads as positive product feedback shaped like a feature item.

note.text: The onboarding guide docs are missing clear setup instructions for new reviewers joining the rollout.

label_rationale: An onboarding document lacks the steps a new reviewer needs; the gap is in written guidance, not in the software itself.

note.text: The reporting dashboard is slow during the weekly cycle and sometimes hits a timeout with high latency for large stores.

label_rationale: The report is usable but slow and occasionally times out under load, which is a performance characteristic rather than a correctness defect.

note.text: The rollout mapping handoff has no owner and the operating routine for the next step is missing from the plan.

label_rationale: A required handoff step in the operating routine has no owner and is undefined, which is a process gap rather than a documentation or access issue.

note.text: The stakeholder left a short note about the overall situation and did not specify any area, owner, or next step.

label_rationale: The note is too vague to place in any specific operational area, so the honest category is other; the tone is a neutral, low-signal remark, and its thinness warrants a review flag.

note.text: The reviewer is frustrated and urgent about the overall situation, but the note never names any specific area, owner, or step to take.

label_rationale: The note cannot be placed in any operational area and the writer is clearly upset while asking for nothing concrete; an other-category note with a negative tone and no follow-up needs review on two counts.

note.text: A store manager requests the standard admin login setup after a routine data sync change; the access step is the primary item for today's scheduled review.

label_rationale: This is an independently labelled case where two operational topics appear together: setting up sign-in and a routine data refresh. The note itself names account access as the primary item, so the human label is access, chosen from what the note emphasises. The tone is a plain scheduling request, so sentiment is neutral.

note.text: The setup guide may be missing a step and it is unclear which section a new reviewer should follow first.

label_rationale: The topic is written setup guidance, but the note is tentative and hedged and never states what is actually wrong, so the low certainty of the note warrants a review flag even though its tone stays neutral.
